entities.name: AGROECOLOGY
entities.description: An alternative paradigm to industrial agriculture aimed at supporting sustainable food systems

entities.name: INDUSTRIAL AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The conventional paradigm of agriculture that agroecology seeks to replace

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH
entities.description: Research focused on increasing food security and well-being

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: WELL-BEING
entities.description: The state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy, a goal of agricultural research

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: Food systems that are environmentally sound and socially just

entities.name: EXTERNAL INPUTS
entities.description: Resources from outside the farm system that farmers seek independence from in agroecology

entities.name: CIRCULAR AGRICULTURE
entities.description: An agricultural approach focusing on recycling nutrients and reducing waste

entities.name: INTENSIFICATION OF AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Increasing the productivity of agriculture, often associated with the Green Revolution

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A set of research technology packages to increase agricultural production

entities.name: FOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: The entire network involved in the production and consumption of food

entities.name: FOOD
entities.description: Nutritious substances needed for health and growth

entities.name: HEALTH
entities.description: A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being

entities.name: PLANETARY BOUNDARIES
entities.description: Environmental limits within which humanity can safely operate
